title: iam-jit OCSF v1.1.0 class 6003 (API Activity) audit event
description:
Cross-product audit-event schema. Every audit-export event from ibounce + kbounce + dbounce conforms to OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework) v1.1.0 class 6003 "API Activity". This schema captures the COMMON SUBSET — the fields every Bounce product emits identically. Per-product extension fields land under `unmapped.iam_jit.ext` and are NOT constrained here (per [[cross-product-agent-parity]] + [[ocsf-audit-schema]]).

The authoritative event-builder lives at src/iam_jit/bouncer/audit_export/event.py (ibounce, Python) and internal/audit/event.go (kbounce, dbounce — Go). Each call site emits the OCSF event identically; only `metadata.product.name`, `unmapped.iam_jit.ext`, and the `activity_id` classifier vary per product.

Why class 6003? It's the closest OCSF class for proxy decisions about service-API calls. AWS SDK requests, K8s API requests, and SQL statements all fit. status_id mapping (per [[ibounce-honest-positioning]]): ALLOW→1, DENY enforced=true→2, DENY enforced=false→1 (cooperative-mode advisory; status_detail carries the deny reason), BYPASS (pause-active)→1.

Schema discipline: additionalProperties:true at every level — OCSF spec growth + per-product `unmapped.iam_jit.ext` extensions are explicitly allowed. Validators that reject unknown fields will see false positives on legitimate events.

version.description: OCSF schema version. Currently "1.1.0" across the suite.

name.description: Per-product name. One of: ibounce, kbounce, dbounce.

vendor_name.description: Shared vendor across the suite — exactly "iam-jit" so a SIEM dashboard scoped to metadata.product.vendor_name == "iam-jit" catches every event.

time.description: Event time in Unix milliseconds (canonical OCSF representation).

class_uid.description: OCSF class id. Locked to 6003 (API Activity) for every iam-jit event.

activity_id.description: OCSF activity_id. 0=Unknown, 1=Create, 2=Read, 3=Update, 4=Delete, 99=Other. Per [[ocsf-audit-schema]]: derived from the service-verb classifier (policy_sentry on AWS, K8s verb on kbounce, SQL type on dbounce). Synthetic AUDIT_DROPPED / ADMIN_ACTION events use 99.

type_uid.description: OCSF type_uid = class_uid * 100 + activity_id. For class 6003: 600300 (Unknown), 600301 (Create), 600302 (Read), 600303 (Update), 600304 (Delete), 600399 (Other).

severity_id.description: OCSF severity_id. Per [[security-team-positioning-safety-not-surveillance]]: default 1 (Informational) for every routine decision. Slice 2 alerts (AUDIT_DROPPED, anomaly_detected) override per the spec table.

status_id.description: OCSF status_id. 1=Success, 2=Failure, 99=Other. Per [[ibounce-honest-positioning]]: cooperative-mode advisory deny lands as Success-with-detail so SIEM Failure dashboards stay accurate.

status_detail.description: Free-text human-readable reason. Carries the deny reason for advisory denies + the pause-bypass note. Per [[security-team-positioning-safety-not-surveillance]]: neutral language.

operation.description: Per-product operation name. ibounce: "<service>:<action>" (e.g. "s3:GetObject"); kbounce: "<verb> <resource>"; dbounce: SQL statement type.

resources.description: OCSF resources[] — one entry per resource the request targets. Empty array when no resource is parseable (e.g. /healthz hits, account-scope AWS calls). Per [[ocsf-audit-schema]]: prefer empty array over synthesised names.

src_endpoint.description: Source endpoint metadata. May be empty when no src info is available (e.g. local-loopback bouncers).

dst_endpoint.description: Destination endpoint metadata. ibounce: AWS service host. kbounce: K8s API host. dbounce: database host.

unmapped.description: OCSF unmapped container. The `iam_jit` sub-key carries every iam-jit-specific field that has no OCSF home.

iam_jit.description: iam-jit extension block. The named fields below are the cross-product common subset. Per-product fields land under `ext` (e.g. ibounce ext.aws_region, kbounce ext.namespace, dbounce ext.dialect).

mode.description: The bouncer mode the decision was evaluated under. ibounce: cooperative|transparent. kbounce: enforce|advisory. dbounce: enforce|advisory.

profile.description: Name of the active profile when the decision fired. null when no profile was selected (rare; default-profile fall-through).

verdict.description: Raw bouncer verdict before OCSF status_id mapping. One of: ALLOW, DENY, BYPASS, PROMPT (sync-deny), or a future verdict the schema doesn't yet pin.

decision_id.description: Monotonic per-process decision counter. Stable for the life of one bouncer process.

enforced.description: Whether the verdict actually altered upstream behavior. False for advisory denies (cooperative-mode flagged but did NOT block).

ext.description: Per-product extension dict. Unconstrained — each Bounce product adds its native fields here (ibounce: aws_region, sigv4_credential_kid; kbounce: namespace, resource_kind; dbounce: dialect, statement_kind).

event_type.description: Set for synthetic events (AUDIT_DROPPED, ADMIN_ACTION). Absent for normal decision events.

admin_action.description: Present only when unmapped.iam_jit.event_type == "ADMIN_ACTION". See admin-action-event.schema.json for the constrained sub-shape.

dropped_count.description: Present only when unmapped.iam_jit.event_type == "AUDIT_DROPPED". Count of decision events the bounded queue dropped.

agent.description: Optional agent-identity block per [[agent-identity-in-audit]]. Present when an agent identity could be resolved from MCP clientinfo, User-Agent, or process tree.
